title: Indoor Voice
description:
Given a string of text, output that same text in lowercase. Assume that the input will be a single line of text.

**Example:**
```
Input:  HELLO, WORLD
Output: hello, world
```
common_mistakes: Using print(input().lower()) without storing the variable
Forgetting that .lower() returns a new string and does not modify in place

title: Playback Speed
description:
Suppose that a user types too quickly and uses ... instead of spaces between words. Write a program that replaces any ... in the input with spaces.

**Example:**
```
Input:  This...is...CS50
Output: This is CS50
```
common_mistakes: Using replace() incorrectly — check argument order: replace(old, new)
Not handling multiple occurrences (str.replace handles all by default)

title: Making Faces
description:
Replace text emoticons :) and :( with their emoji equivalents 🙂 and 🙁.

**Example:**
```
Input:  Hello, world :)
Output: Hello, world 🙂
```
common_mistakes: Only replacing one of the two emoticons

title: Einstein
description:
Prompt the user for mass in kilograms (as an integer), then output the equivalent number of Joules using E = mc², where c = 300,000,000 m/s.

**Example:**
```
m: 1
1.0 Joule
```
Note: output exactly that many Joules with no trailing zeros for whole numbers.
common_mistakes: Using the wrong value for c
Not converting the input string to int before multiplication

title: Tip Calculator
description:
Complete the functions in the tip calculator program. Implement `dollars_to_float` (strips `$` prefix and converts to float) and `percent_to_float` (strips `%` suffix and converts to float).

**Example:**
```
How much was the meal? $50.00
What percentage would you like to tip? 15%
Leave $7.50
```
common_mistakes: Stripping the wrong character or using the wrong slice index
Forgetting to divide the percentage by 100 before multiplying
